Key Features

6K Cinematic Video

Uncompromised 6K/24p and 5.9K/30p video recording. Capture more data than ever before with full-frame recording tailored for high-end cinema.

Dual Native ISO

A revolutionary sensor design minimises noise and maximises image quality from low to high sensitivity. Experience pristine footage even in extreme low-light.

14+ Stops V-Log

Enjoy 14+ stops of dynamic range, rivalling high-end cinema cameras. Capture accurate colour reproduction and subtle gradations from dark to light.

Unlimited Recording Time

A built-in cooling fan ensures thermal management is never an issue. The S1H supports unlimited video recording in all modes without overheating.

5-Axis Dual I.S. 2

Shoot handheld with confidence. Our advanced stabilization system offers up to 6.5 stops of compensation, letting you achieve smooth footage anywhere.

Weather-Sealed Design

Built for professionals. The rugged, splash, dust, and freeze-resistant body is designed to withstand the harshest shooting environments down to -10°C.

Autofocus
4.2

The ultimate Netflix Approved workhorse

David P. — Verified Buyer

I upgraded from the GH5 and the full frame leap is massive. The 6K 24p mode holds so much detail, and the V-Log color science makes matching to Arri/RED cameras incredibly easy. Having a proper cooling fan means I never have to worry about overheating on long shoots.

124 people found this helpful

Incredible image, but it's heavy

Sarah T. — Verified Buyer

The image quality and dual native ISO blow me away every time I use it. However, this is not a lightweight hybrid camera. It's essentially a cinema camera in a mirrorless body. The weight is considerable if you're flying it on a standard gimbal.

89 people found this helpful
Filter reviews
Rating
Contains Media
Sort by:
Most Helpful

Game changer for indie filmmakers

2 months ago
James K.
Verified Buyer

This camera bridged the gap between my DSLR days and stepping up to true production cinema bodies. The IBIS is so good I rarely use a gimbal for documentary work anymore. The robust codec options make post-production an absolute breeze.

Was this helpful?

Best mirrorless for video, bar none

4 months ago
Alex R.
Verified Buyer

I shoot commercial real estate and events. The 10-bit color depth and lack of a recording limit are the reasons I switched from Sony. No more worrying during a 3-hour conference recording. Menus are also the best out of any brand I&apos;ve used.

Was this helpful?

Autofocus is just okay

6 months ago
Chris M.
Verified Buyer

If you pull focus manually, this is a 5-star camera. But if you rely on continuous autofocus during run-and-gun shoots, the DFD contrast-detect AF will hunt occasionally. It&apos;s totally fine for studio work, but tracking fast subjects isn&apos;t its strong suit.

Was this helpful?
